Description: Composite risk score that aggregates multiple crisis exposure factors into a single broad indicator of overall daily risk for the stock
A score representing the degree of hedge fund ownership crowding in the stock; higher crowding suggests increased risk of volatility from potential forced selling or deleveraging
A measure of the stock’s sensitivity (beta) to changes in high-yield credit spreads, indicating potential outperformance during credit spread spikes
Sensitivity (beta) of the stock’s returns to changes in long-term interest rates, measuring potential performance as interest rates move
A ratio (commonly long-term debt to equity) that reflects the financial leverage of the company, with higher values indicating greater risk in deteriorating credit environments
Probability of default for the company as implied by a Merton credit risk model, which uses equity and market data to estimate risk of default
Percentage of company sales generated in European markets, indicating sensitivity to European economic or crisis conditions
Proportion of cash and short-term investments relative to total assets, indicating the firm’s liquidity and providing a buffer against funding stress
